g.text: 802 Bankview Dr, Drumheller, Alberta | File VAL251026
Limited data available for Drumheller as CMHC does not track this market. Southern and rural Alberta multifamily
markets remain structurally undersupplied, with low vacancy, steady rent growth and strong demographic support.
Calgary’s temporary vacancy lift reflects new supply absorption, while secondary cities and rural communities,
particularly Drumheller, continue to exhibit tight conditions due to limited inventory and persistent demand.
• Vacancy has dropped from mid-single digits to near 1% in recent years.
• Rising rents across all unit types.
• Very limited availability of modern purpose-built product.
• Strong population inflows throughout Southern Alberta.
• Affordability advantage relative to major Canadian metros.
• Structural mismatch between rental demand and new supply, especially outside Calgary.
• Growing investor interest in secondary and rural markets where yields remain attractive.
• Increasing renovation and repositioning activity in older stock due to replacement-cost pressures.
